title: azalea westlandii
intro: Azalea westlandii, also known as the Western Azalea, is a flowering plant native to the Pacific coast of North America, characterized by its vibrant pink, purple, or white blooms. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Azalea westlandii include reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
